threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still stand on the platform waiting for the Tube to magically arrive instead of checking the real-time updates on your phone.
Caught a glimpse of a rat casually riding the backseat of the 35 last Saturday. It was totally unfazed by the chaos around it.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I realized I was really a Neuköllner? Watching a group of kids build a makeshift skate ramp in the playground with just a couple of crates and a plank.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Friedrichshain will you find a 24-hour tram that becomes a moving party on weekends, with spontaneous DJ sets and late-night snacks.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s local if they can name the taco truck that replaced the old pupusas joint on 24th. It's been a hot topic since last fall.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the Muni stuck behind a street performer doing a fire-breathing act at 6th & Market. Driver just sighed and turned up the radio.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists crowd the benches at Plaça del Sol, oblivious to the locals’ secret spots just a few steps away. They miss out on the best shadows and stories.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell they're new by the way they still use Google Maps for every street—look up and you'll see locals navigating by memory.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still look confused when the bus suddenly stops and everyone leaps off at the random unmarked stop. That’s just how Dalston works.
The other night, saw a bloke chatting up a stray cat at the bus stop—kept saying he understood its life struggles. Bizarrely touching.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: I knew I was home when I heard the sound of a street musician mixing traditional tunes with techno at Sonnenallee. Only here, right?

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Friedrichshain can you overhear a heated debate about the best vegan döner while standing next to a street artist painting a mural of a cat in a spacesuit.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know they’re local when they take the 14 Mission and complain about the bus delays like it’s a rite of passage, not a shock.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy in a tuxedo trying to navigate the Muni with a pet iguana. No one batted an eye. Only in SF.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think they can order a 'café con leche' any time, but locals know it's strictly breakfast—or you're just asking for a baffled look.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can spot a recent arrival by their bewildered expression while trying to decipher the metro map during rush hour. Always looking lost.
